description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof covered in a tan material, likely thatch or shingles, with a prominent ridge beam running along the top. The front facade displays a timber-framed design with visible wooden beams and infill panels, evoking a traditional or medieval European style. A single wooden door is present on the front, but there are no visible windows on any side of the structure. The walls are constructed from dark, textured material bordered by lighter stone or plaster, giving the house a rustic appearance. The side and rear views show plain, unadorned walls beneath the expansive roof, emphasizing the simplicity of the design. The overall structure is compact, with a single story and a straightforward, functional layout.
description: A compact, timber-framed house with a steep gable roof, dark textured walls, and a single wooden door, lacking visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the timber-framed facade, a single wooden door, and the steep gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the large tan roof, with plain dark walls and no visible openings.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring a plain dark wall beneath the gable roof, with no doors or windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the large roof and plain wall.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door set within a timber-framed facade, no steps, porch, or decorative elements
